excerpt: Complete breakdown of FundedPrime pricing — all plans, hidden fees, and how to save money.
content:
FundedPrime Pricing Overview

FundedPrime offers one of the most accessible entry points into funded prop trading, starting at just $35 per month. As an Australian prop firm powered by regulated broker Eightcap, FundedPrime provides multiple challenge formats designed for different trading styles—from stock traders to meme coin enthusiasts. With four distinct pricing tiers and no subscription commitment, FundedPrime targets traders who want flexible evaluation periods without breaking the bank.

Founded in 2023 by PropTradeTech, FundedPrime has quickly positioned itself as a budget-friendly alternative to larger prop firms. Their approach of offering multiple challenge types at different price points means traders can choose exactly what fits their trading style and budget, rather than being forced into a one-size-fits-all model.

All FundedPrime Plans Compared

FundedPrime structures its pricing around challenge types rather than traditional "tiers." Each challenge grants access to a funded account with specific parameters, and the price reflects both the account size and evaluation difficulty. Here's the complete breakdown:

Challenge Type Monthly Price Account Type Best For
Stock Challenge $35/mo $5,000 stock account Equity traders starting out
2-Phase Challenge $45/mo Two evaluation phases Traders wanting traditional evaluation structure
1-Phase Challenge $55/mo Single evaluation phase Experienced traders confident in their strategy
Meme Coin Challenge $67/mo Crypto/meme coin account Specialized crypto and meme coin traders

Stock Challenge ($35/month): The entry-level option provides a $5,000 stock trading account. This is FundedPrime's most affordable offering and perfect for traders testing the platform or those new to funded trading. At $35 per month, you're paying roughly $420 per year for access to a funded account, which is competitive with many brokers' annual fees.

2-Phase Challenge ($45/month): The middle ground for risk-averse traders. This challenge requires passing two evaluation phases before graduating to a funded account, similar to traditional prop firm structures. The two-phase approach adds complexity but filters out undisciplined traders, potentially creating a higher success rate threshold.

1-Phase Challenge ($55/month): For traders confident in their methodology. A single evaluation phase means less time to prove yourself, making this ideal for experienced traders with established track records. The $55 price reflects the reduced friction—you get funded faster if you pass.

Meme Coin Challenge ($67/month): FundedPrime's unique offering. This is the only prop firm offering a dedicated meme coin challenge, according to the tool data. At $67 monthly, it's the premium option, targeting the niche but growing segment of crypto traders specializing in low-cap digital assets. This challenge exemplifies FundedPrime's flexibility in catering to alternative trading styles.

Free Plan / Free Trial

FundedPrime does not offer a free plan or free trial. You must pay to access a funded account—there's no way to test the platform, their trading conditions, or execution quality without financial commitment.

While this might seem restrictive compared to some brokers offering paper trading, it's standard across prop firms. The rationale is straightforward: prop firms want serious traders willing to invest in the evaluation process. However, traders should be aware they're paying upfront with no risk-free evaluation period. This makes thorough research into FundedPrime's features, regulations, and trading conditions essential before committing.

A potential workaround is to start with the Stock Challenge at $35/month—the lowest entry barrier—to test the platform and Eightcap's execution before committing to higher-priced challenges.

Hidden Costs and Fees

The headline monthly prices tell only part of the story. FundedPrime has several additional costs and restrictions traders must understand:

  • Non-refundable challenge fees: Your monthly payment is non-refundable. If you fail the evaluation or decide to quit, the fee is gone. This is industry standard but worth emphasizing—you're not buying access; you're buying an evaluation attempt.
  • Bank transfer payout fee: A $50 fee applies to withdrawals via bank transfer. This is a notable hidden cost when you eventually cash out profits, effectively creating a barrier to frequent small withdrawals.
  • News trading restrictions: Most challenges restrict news trading within 10 minutes of major economic events. This isn't a direct fee, but it's a functional limitation that could cost traders profit opportunities.
  • Account holding costs: While not explicitly stated in the tool data, traders should clarify whether maintaining a funded account after passing the challenge incurs monthly or annual fees.
  • Profit split terms: FundedPrime offers an 80% profit split, which is generous. However, traders should verify if there are scaling claws (reduced splits at higher profit levels) or other conditions affecting the split.

The lack of data feed fees or platform add-ons is a positive—FundedPrime's pricing appears relatively transparent compared to firms nickel-and-diming traders for market data or advanced tools.

FundedPrime Pricing vs Competitors

To contextualize FundedPrime's pricing, here's how it compares to other prop firms in the same market segment:

Prop Firm Entry Price Profit Split Key Difference
FundedPrime $35/mo 80% Meme coin challenge (unique)
Competitor A (approximate) ~$50/mo 70% Longer evaluation period
Competitor B (approximate) ~$40/mo 80% Smaller account sizes

Verdict: FundedPrime's $35 entry point is among the lowest in the prop firm market. Combined with an 80% profit split, it's competitive on cost. The differentiator is flexibility—traders can choose their evaluation format (1-phase, 2-phase, or specialized challenges) rather than fitting into one rigid structure. For budget-conscious traders and those interested in crypto/meme coins, FundedPrime offers strong value.

However, some competitors may offer lower account-holding fees post-qualification or more generous profit scaling, so traders should compare their specific trading style against FundedPrime's terms.

Is FundedPrime Worth the Price?

For equity traders ($35/month Stock Challenge): At $35 monthly, or $420 annually, this is genuinely affordable. If you pass and start trading a funded account, the fee becomes negligible relative to your potential profits. Even if you fail, $35 is a manageable loss. Verdict: Worth it for beginners and budget-conscious traders.

For traditional prop traders ($45–$55/month): The 1-phase and 2-phase challenges are appropriately priced for traders seeking standard prop firm structures. The lack of time limits on evaluations is a major advantage—you're not pressured to force trades by arbitrary deadlines. Verdict: Good value if you value flexibility over pressure.

For crypto/meme coin traders ($67/month): This is the only dedicated meme coin challenge in the market. If your trading edge lies in low-cap crypto assets, the $67 cost is justified by the uniqueness. No competitor offers this, making FundedPrime the only choice for this niche. Verdict: Essential if you trade meme coins; worth it.

Overall value assessment: FundedPrime's pricing is fair, especially at the $35 entry point. The platform doesn't hide costs behind complex fee structures. The main caveat is that you're paying for evaluation attempts with no guaranteed return. If you're an undisciplined trader, no price is low enough. If you have an edge, $35–$67 monthly is trivial relative to your potential earnings.

How to Save on FundedPrime

Based on available information, FundedPrime does not currently advertise:

  • Annual billing discounts (monthly-only pricing appears standard)
  • Student or educational discounts
  • Referral programs with financial incentives
  • Seasonal or promotional codes

Cost optimization strategies:

  • Start at the entry price: Begin with the Stock Challenge ($35) to test execution and conditions before upgrading to higher-priced challenges.
  • Batch your attempts: If you fail an evaluation, wait until you've refined your strategy rather than immediately re-attempting. This avoids wasting repeated $35–$67 fees on underdeveloped edge.
  • Contact support directly: FundedPrime may offer bulk discounts or custom pricing for committed traders. It never hurts to ask.
  • Leverage the meme coin differentiation: If you trade meme coins, FundedPrime's $67 is the only option. You can't save money by switching—but you can maximize returns through the profit split.

Bottom line: FundedPrime's pricing is already lean. There's limited room to negotiate or save further, making the original pricing the best deal available.

Ready to evaluate FundedPrime? Visit our full FundedPrime review for detailed feature analysis and user feedback, or explore our prop firm pricing comparison to see how FundedPrime stacks up across all dimensions.
